messages.content:
Which of the following acts will attract criminal liability for collusion with a foreign country or with external elements to endanger national security (勾結外國或者境外勢力危害國家安全罪)?
(1) Spying for a foreign country
(2) Requesting a foreign organisation outside the Mainland, Hong Kong and Macao to impose sanctions against the Hong Kong SAR
(3) Taking charge of a terrorist organisation

(1) Spying for a foreign country may attract criminal liability for collusion with a foreign country or with external elements to endanger national security.
(2) Requesting a foreign organisation outside the Mainland, Hong Kong, and Macao to impose sanctions against the Hong Kong SAR may attract criminal liability for collusion with a foreign country or with external elements to endanger national security.
(3) Taking charge of a terrorist organisation may attract criminal liability for terrorism-related offenses but may not directly fall under the category of collusion with a foreign country or with external elements to endanger national security.

Mr. Breach publishes a post on Instagram the message “Hongkongers, let’s build our own nation (香港人建國)”. Which of the following statements in relation to the Law of the People’s Republic of China on Safeguarding National Security in the Hong Kong SAR (中華人民共和國香港特別行政區維護國家安全法) (“NSL”) is correct?
Question 3 Answer

a.
Mr. Breach is not liable for any offence under the NSL at all.

b.
If Mr. A is not a permanent resident of the Hong Kong SAR, then NSL will not apply to Mr. Breach.

c.
Mr. Breach commits the offence of incitement to secession (煽動分裂國家) under Articles 20 and 21 of the NSL.

d.
Mr. Breach commits the offence of collusion with a foreign country or with external elements to endanger national security (勾結外國或者境外勢力危害國家安全) under Article 29 of the NSL.

d.
Mr. Breach commits the offence of collusion with a foreign country or with external elements to endanger national security (勾結外國或者境外勢力危害國家安全) under Article 29 of the NSL.

Mr. A operates a website for the campaign of requesting the government of a foreign country, namely Country C, to impose sanctions against senior officials of the Hong Kong SAR Government. The website uses the slogan “Liberate Hong Kong, Revolution of Our Times” (光復香港，時代革命) as background on every webpage. While the website is accessible in Country C only (i.e. not in Hong Kong), the server of the website is also situated in Country C. Which of the following statements in relation to the Law of the People’s Republic of China on Safeguarding National Security in the Hong Kong SAR (中華人民共和國香港特別行政區維護國家安全法) (“NSL”) is false?
Question 4 Answer

a.
If Mr. A is not a permanent resident of the Hong Kong SAR, NSL still applies to Mr. A.

b.
Mr. A commits the offence of incitement to secession (煽動分裂國家) under Articles 20 and 21 of the NSL.

c.
Mr. A commits the offence of collusion with a foreign country or with external elements to endanger national security (勾結外國或者境外勢力危害國家安全) under Article 29 of the NSL.

d.
Mr. A is not liable for any offence under the NSL because the website is not accessible in Hong Kong.

d.
Mr. A is not liable for any offence under the NSL because the website is not accessible in Hong Kong.

Mr. Breach commits incitement to secession (煽動分裂國家) under the Law of the People’s Republic of China on Safeguarding National Security in the Hong Kong SAR (中華人民共和國香港特別行政區維護國家安全法) (“NSL”)? Upon which of the following defence(s) can Mr. Breach rely?
Question 5 Answer

a.
No person in fact commits any actual act of secession

b.
None of the other choices

c.
Mr. Breach is below 18 years old

d.
Mr. Breach exercises his right to freedom of speech as protected by the Basic Law

b.
None of the other choices.

Mr. X posted in Silver Forum (an online forum to which any registered internet user can access) the following message, “Hey! Let’s fight the police and revenge. What we need to do is simply stab any uniformed police officer you see in the street! They cannot stop us all! Let’s gather at Exit A of Causeway Bay MTR Station at 3 p.m. tomorrow and action! We have to free Hong Kong with our hands!” Ms. Y shared Mr. X’s post on her Instagram. No one shows up and no one attacks any police officer at the end of the day. Which of the following statements is correct?
Question 7 Answer

a.
Mr. X is not liable for any criminal offence.

b.
Both Mr. X and Ms. Y commits Incitement to commission of a terrorist activity (煽動實施恐怖活動) under the National Security Law.

c.
Ms. Y is not liable for any criminal offence.

d.
Both Mr. X and Ms. Y commits the offence of collusion with a foreign country or with external elements to endanger national security (勾結外國或者境外勢力危害國家安全) under the National Security Law.

b.
Both Mr. X and Ms. Y commit Incitement to the commission of a terrorist activity (煽動實施恐怖活動) under the National Security Law.

The Office for Safeguarding National Security of the Central People’s Government in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region shall be funded by _______ .
Question 9 Answer

a.
the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region Government

b.
the Central People’s Government

c.
the State Council

d.
The Liaison Office of the Central People's Government in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region

b.
the Central People's Government.

Which of the following is NOT the duty and function of the Committee for Safeguarding National Security (維護國家安全委員會)?

a.
Analysing and assessing developments in relation to safeguarding national security in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region, making work plans, and formulating policies for safeguarding national security in the Hong Kong SAR.

b.
Recommending the size of People’s Liberation Army in Hong Kong

c.
Coordinating major work and significant operations for safeguarding national security in the Hong Kong SAR.

d.
Advancing the development of the legal system and enforcement mechanisms of the Hong Kong SAR for safeguarding national security

b.
Recommending the size of People's Liberation Army in Hong Kong.

Which of the following statements about the Law of the People’s Republic of China on Safeguarding National Security in the Hong Kong SAR (中華人民共和國香港特別行政區維護國家安全法) (“NSL”) is true?
Question 12 Answer

a.
NSL does not apply to churches in Hong Kong

b.
NSL applies to international non-governmental organizations (NGOs), local charitable organizations and churches in Hong Kong.

c.
NSL does not apply to local charitable organizations in Hong Kong

d.
NSL does not apply to international non-governmental organizations (NGOs) in Hong Kong

b.
NSL applies to international non-governmental organizations (NGOs), local charitable organizations, and churches in Hong Kong.

The principle of the rule of law shall be adhered to in ______ . A person who commits an act which constitutes an offence under the law shall be convicted and punished in accordance with the law. No one shall be convicted and punished for an act which does not constitute an offence under the law.
(1) preventing endangering national security
(2) suppressing endangering national security
(3) imposing punishment for offences endangering national security
(3) imposing punishment for offences endangering national security

Which of the following statements about the Law of the People’s Republic of China on Safeguarding National Security in the Hong Kong SAR (中華人民共和國香港特別行政區維護國家安全法) (“NSL”) is/are true?
Question 14 Answer

a.
The National Security Adviser (國家安全事務顧問) of the Committee for Safeguarding National Security (維護國家安全委員會) shall be appointed by the Chief Executive of the Hong Kong SAR.

b.
Decisions made by the Committee for Safeguarding National Security (維護國家安全委員會) shall not be amenable to judicial review.

c.
The Court of Final Appeal (CFA) has the final interpretation of the NSL

d.
The enactment of the NSL discharges the Hong Kong SAR’s obligation under Article 23 of the Basic Law.

b.
Decisions made by the Committee for Safeguarding National Security (維護國家安全委員會) shall not be amenable to judicial review.

The principle of the rule of law shall be adhered to in ______ . A person who commits an act which constitutes an offence under the law shall be convicted and punished in accordance with the law. No one shall be convicted and punished for an act which does not constitute an offence under the law.
(1) preventing endangering national security
(2) suppressing endangering national security
(3) imposing punishment for offences endangering national security
Question 13 Answer

a.
(1), (3)

b.
(1), (2), (3)

c.
(1), (4)

d.
(2), (3)

The staff of the Office for Safeguarding National Security of the Central People’s Government in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region ______ abide by the laws of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region and ______ abide by national laws.
Question 16 Answer

a.
shall, shall not

b.
shall not, shall

c.
shall not, shall not

d.
shall, shall

b.
shall not, shall

To which of the following entities does the Law of the People’s Republic of China on Safeguarding National Security in the Hong Kong SAR (中華人民共和國香港特別行政區維護國家安全法) apply?
(1) A Hong Kong permanent resident residing in UK
(2) A company incorporated in Bermuda carrying on business in Hong Kong
(3) A Hong Kong incorporated company carrying on business in US
Question 17 Answer

a.
(1), (2) and (3)

b.
(1) and (3)

c.
(1) and (2)

d.
(3) only

The Chief Executive shall designate a number of judges from ______ , and may also designate a number of judges from deputy judges or recorders, to handle cases concerning offence endangering national security.
Question 18 Answer

a.
the judges of the Court of First Instance and the Court of Appeal of the High Court

b.
the judges of the Court of First Instance

c.
the judges of the District Court, the judges of the Court of First Instance and the Court of Appeal of the High Court

d.
the magistrates, the judges of the District Court, the judges of the Court of First Instance and the Court of Appeal of the High Court, and the judges of the Court of Final Appeal

a.
the judges of the Court of First Instance and the Court of Appeal of the High Court

The Hong Kong National Security Law is enacted in accordance with _______.
Question 19 Answer

a.
the Constitution of the People’s Republic of China and article 23 of the Basic Law of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of the People’s Republic of China.

b.
the Constitution of the People’s Republic of China.

c.
the Constitution of the People’s Republic of China, the Basic Law of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of the People’s Republic of China, and the Decision of the National People’s Congress on Establishing and Improving the Legal System and Enforcement Mechanisms for Safeguarding National Security in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region.

d.
the Constitution of the People’s Republic of China and the Basic Law of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of the People’s Republic of China.

c.
the Constitution of the People's Republic of China, the Basic Law of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of the People's Republic of China, and the Decision of the National People's Congress on Establishing and Improving the Legal System and Enforcement Mechanisms for Safeguarding National Security in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region.

The provisions in _______ of the Basic Law of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region on the legal status of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region are the fundamental provisions in the Basic Law.
Question 20 Answer

a.
Articles 1 and 23

b.
Articles 1

c.
Articles 1 and 12

d.
Articles 1 and 2

d.
Articles 1 and 2
briefly introduce LGBTQ+ movement

The LGBTQ+ movement, also known as the queer or gay rights movement, aims to advocate for the rights, acceptance, and equality of individuals who identify as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, or queer (LGBTQ+). It is a global social and political movement that seeks to challenge discrimination, prejudice, and violence against LGBTQ+ people.

The movement encompasses a wide range of issues, including legal rights, anti-discrimination policies, marriage equality, transgender rights, healthcare access, and societal acceptance. Activists and organizations within the LGBTQ+ movement work towards achieving these goals through various means, such as lobbying for legislative changes, participating in protests and pride parades, raising awareness, providing support and resources, and fostering inclusive communities.

Over the years, the LGBTQ+ movement has achieved significant milestones, including the decriminalization of homosexuality in many countries, the recognition of same-sex marriage, and improvements in LGBTQ+ rights protection. However, there are still ongoing challenges and struggles faced by the community, such as discrimination, violence, and lack of legal protection and rights in certain regions.

The LGBTQ+ movement plays a crucial role in promoting visibility, acceptance, and equality for LGBTQ+ individuals, and continues to push for progress and social change to create a more inclusive and equitable society for all.

What independent power shall the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region be vested with?
Question 2 Answer

a.
executive power

b.
judicial power, including that of final adjudication

c.
legislative power

d.
judicial power

b.
judicial power, including that of final adjudication.

Where provisions of the local laws of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region are inconsistent with the Hong Kong National Security law, ______ shall prevail.
Question 3 Answer

a.
the national laws

b.
the Hong Kong National Security Law

c.
the local laws of the Region

d.
the final judgment of the Court of Final Appeal

b.
the Hong Kong National Security Law.

On 1 July (i.e. the Anniversary Day of Establishment of the Hong Kong SAR) Mr A displayed in public a banner with the slogan “Liberate Hong Kong, Revolution of our Times” (光復香港 時代革命) printed on it. Which of the following national security offences did Mr A commit?
Question 5 Answer

a.
Incitement of Secession (煽惑分裂國家罪)

b.
Secession (分裂國家罪)

c.
Incitement of Terrorist Activities (煽惑恐怖活動罪)

d.
Subversion (顛覆國家政權罪)

b.
Secession (分裂國家罪)

Which of the following statements about the Law of the People’s Republic of China on Safeguarding National Security in the Hong Kong SAR (中華人民共和國香港特別行政區維護國家安全法) (“NSL”) is/are correct?
Question 7 Answer

a.
A company can be liable for offences under the NSL.

b.
The presumption of innocence does not apply when one is prosecuted for criminal offences under the NSL.

c.
The standard of proof of NSL offence is the same as that of civil case, i.e. on the balance of probabilities

d.
When any provisions of the NSL is inconsistent with the Basic Law, the NSL provisions shall prevail.

a.
A company can be liable for offences under the NSL.

b.
The presumption of innocence does not apply when one is prosecuted for criminal offences under the NSL.

Which of the following are NOT part of the duties and functions of the Committee for Safeguarding National Security (維護國家安全委員會)?
Question 12 Answer

a.
Coordinating major work and significant operations for safeguarding national security in the Hong Kong SAR.

b.
Collecting and analysing intelligence and information concerning national security.

c.
Advancing the development of the legal system and enforcement mechanisms of the Hong Kong SAR for safeguarding national security.

d.
Analysing and assessing developments in relation to safeguarding national security in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region, making work plans, and formulating policies for safeguarding national security in the Hong Kong SAR

c.
Advancing the development of the legal system and enforcement mechanisms of the Hong Kong SAR for safeguarding national security.
